title: Lolium bromoides
intro: Lolium bromoides, also known as meadow brome, is a perennial grass species belonging to the Poaceae family, native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lolium bromoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Meadow brome is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in the form of dried flowers and leaves, to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Lolium bromoides is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae, which is a group of cool-season grasses. The genus Lolium is a group of annual and perennial grasses, and Lolium bromoides is one of the species within this genus. Lolium bromoides is closely related to other Lolium species, such as Lolium multiflorum and Lolium temulentum. The classification of Lolium bromoides is further divided into various botanical and agricultural classifications.

constituents: Lolium bromoides, also known as cheagrass, is a grass species with various potential health benefits. Research on Lolium bromoides reveals its active constituents, which include flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid. These compounds may have antioxidant properties and contribute to the plant's potential health benefits. Additionally, Lolium bromoides contains other bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and triterpenes. Further studies are needed to fully understand the active constituents and their effects on human health.
